business_goals: Enable system builders to integrate systems on time and within costs
Drive down the cost of command and control systems
Facilitate 'plug-and-play' integration regardless of component location
technical_constraints: Must use Common Object Request Broker Architecture (CORBA)
Focus on interface definition rather than direct component reuse

phase_1_discovery.initial_documentation_status: The reference architecture was partially documented. The team had developed high-level Interface Design Language (IDL) and completed approximately one-fourth of the IDL for the 12 identified sub-domains. The specification was not complete.
evaluator_inquiry_questions: What are the specific quality attribute goals for the system?
How does the architecture support reasoning about tradeoffs between competing attributes like performance and reliability?

hardware_view_description: Ground-based command and control hardware infrastructure
runtime_constraints: Interoperability across autonomous and directed components

questions: How does the system ensure semantic integration between components, not just syntactic connection?
What mechanisms exist to guarantee real-time performance across the distributed middleware?

raw_candidate_scenarios.description: A system integrator incorporates a new vendor component into the system.
The system must operate continuously despite a partial network failure.

architectural_approaches.description: Implementation of Common Object Request Broker Architecture to facilitate communication between distributed components in a heterogeneous environment.

addressed_attributes.rationale: CORBA provides a standardized communication layer that allows components to interact regardless of their physical location or hardware platform.
By decoupling components through a middleware layer, individual parts of the system can be updated or replaced with minimal impact on the rest of the system.

architectural_approaches.description: Utilization of object-oriented principles (encapsulation, inheritance, polymorphism) to structure the system components.

addressed_attributes.rationale: OO technology promotes high cohesion and low coupling, making it easier to modify internal component logic without affecting external callers.

architectural_approaches.description: Focusing on the definition of component interfaces using Interface Definition Language (IDL) rather than direct implementation reuse.

addressed_attributes.rationale: Standardized IDL definitions ensure that different vendors can develop compatible components that plug into the reference architecture.
Focusing on interfaces allows for 'plug-and-play' functionality where components can be swapped as long as they adhere to the defined IDL.

children.scenario_description: A system integrator incorporates a new vendor component into the system.
The system must operate continuously despite a partial network failure.
End-to-end message delivery under 50ms under normal operating conditions.
Update component internal logic without requiring changes to the IDL or affecting other components.

scenarios.scenario_text: A system integrator incorporates a new vendor component into the system to achieve plug-and-play integration via standardized IDL interfaces.
The system must maintain continuous operation and message delivery despite a partial network failure in the middleware layer.
The system ensures end-to-end message delivery under 50ms under normal operating conditions to meet real-time constraints.
A developer updates a component's internal logic; the change must not require updates to the IDL or affect other components in the heterogeneous environment.

scenario_reference.text: A system integrator incorporates a new vendor component into the system to achieve plug-and-play integration via standardized IDL interfaces.

analysis_records.stimulus: Addition of a heterogeneous vendor component
analysis_records.response_measure: Integration completed without modifying existing core middleware infrastructure
architectural_decisions: CORBA IDL (Interface Definition Language) for service contracts
analysis_records.reasoning: By using IDL, the architecture decouples the interface from the implementation language and platform, facilitating the 'plug-and-play' requirement.

non_risks.rationale: CORBA's IDL provides a robust abstraction layer that prevents vendor lock-in at the source code level.

scenario_reference.text: The system ensures end-to-end message delivery under 50ms under normal operating conditions to meet real-time constraints.

analysis_records.stimulus: High-frequency message traffic between distributed objects
analysis_records.reasoning: CORBA introduces a middleware layer that requires data translation (marshalling) and network traversal, which adds overhead compared to direct memory access or specialized protocols.

risks.rationale: Complex IDL data structures may lead to serialization latencies that exceed the 50ms threshold in high-load scenarios.

scenario_reference.text: The system must maintain continuous operation and message delivery despite a partial network failure in the middleware layer.

analysis_records.stimulus: Loss of connection to a subset of nodes
analysis_records.response_measure: Continuous message delivery via alternative paths or failover
analysis_records.reasoning: Standard CORBA implementations often rely on a centralized Naming Service which can become a single point of failure unless specialized Fault-Tolerant (FT-CORBA) extensions are used.

risks.rationale: If the CORBA Naming Service or specific ORB implementation is not redundant, a network partition could isolate large segments of the system.

sensitivity_points.parameter: Timeout and Retry policies in ORB
